016package org.consensusj.analytics.util.collector;
017
018import org.slf4j.Logger;
019import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;
020
021import java.util.AbstractQueue;
022import java.util.Comparator;
023import java.util.List;
024import java.util.PriorityQueue;
025import java.util.function.BinaryOperator;
026import java.util.function.Function;
027import java.util.stream.Collectors;
028
029/**
030 * Accumulates the {@code n} largest slices of a stream of objects of type {@code <E>}. Uses a {@link PriorityQueue}
031 * implementation of {@link AbstractQueue} internally.
032 * <p>
033 * This class is <b>not</b> thread-safe and is <b>not</b> designed for use with a {@link java.util.stream.Collector.Characteristics#CONCURRENT}
034 * {@link java.util.stream.Collector}. Switching the {@link AbstractQueue} implementation to {@link java.util.concurrent.PriorityBlockingQueue}
035 * will work (with reduced performance) but the implementations of {@link #accumulate(Object)}, {@link #combine(LargestSliceAccumulator)},
036 * and {@link #drain()} are not thread-safe. Even if this class were thread-safe, enabling {@code CONCURRENT}
037 * in a {@code Collector} using this class would <b>reduce</b> performance because multiple threads would be trying to merge Objects
038 * into the same accumulator. The optimal way to use this class is with one accumulator per thread
039 * and this is what Collector will do without the {@code CONCURRENT} flag.
040 * 
041 * @param <E> must be comparable because it is the second sort field
042 * @param <N> numeric type for Slice Size
043 */
044public final class LargestSliceAccumulator<E, N extends Number & Comparable<? super N>>  {
045    private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(LargestSliceAccumulator.class);
046    private final int n;  // Maximum number of slices to track
047    private final Function<E, N> sliceSizeExtractor;
048    private final BinaryOperator<N> additionOperator;
049    private final AbstractQueue<E> sliceQueue;
050    private final Comparator<E> comparator;
051    private N otherTotal;
052    
053    /**
054     * Construct
055     * @param n maximum number of keys (addresses) to track
056     * @param sliceSizeExtractor Function to compute the slice size
057     * @param zero The value zero for type N
058     * @param additionOperator binary addition operator for type N
059     */
060    public LargestSliceAccumulator(int n,
061                                   Function<E, N> sliceSizeExtractor,
062                                   N zero,
063                                   BinaryOperator<N> additionOperator) {
064        if (n < 1) {
065            throw new IllegalArgumentException("parameter must be 1 or greater");
066        }
067        this.n = n;
068        this.sliceSizeExtractor = sliceSizeExtractor;
069        this.additionOperator = additionOperator;
070        this.otherTotal = zero;
071        this.comparator = Comparator.comparing(sliceSizeExtractor);
072        log.trace("Creating accum queue");
073        sliceQueue = new PriorityQueue<>(n, Comparator.comparing(sliceSizeExtractor));
074    }
075
076    /**
077     * Add a new slice to the accumulator
078     *
079     * @param newSlice slice to accumulate
080     */
081    void accumulate(E newSlice) {
082        //log.trace("accumulating slice of size: {}", sliceSizeExtractor.apply(newSlice).doubleValue());
083        sliceQueue.add(newSlice);
084        drain();    // Remove extra elements.
085    }
086
087    /**
088     * Combine two accumulators
089     *
090     * @param other the other accumulator
091     * @return the combined accumulator (this)
092     */
093    LargestSliceAccumulator<E, N> combine(LargestSliceAccumulator<E, N > other) {
094        other.sliceQueue.forEach(this::accumulate);
095        otherTotal = plus(otherTotal, other.otherTotal);
096        return this;
097    }
098
099    // It's simpler (and possibly faster) to let the queue do the size comparison
100    // and just remove extra elements rather than implement our own checks _before_
101    // adding to the queue.
102    private void drain() {
103        while (sliceQueue.size() > n) {
104            E removed = sliceQueue.poll();
105            if (removed != null) {
106                otherTotal = plus(otherTotal, sliceSizeExtractor.apply(removed));
107            }
108        }
109    }
110    
111    /**
112     * Sort the sliceQueue and return as a {@link List}.
113     * Normally, This should only be called by the Collector finisher function.
114     * 
115     * @return List of slices sorted by their slice size (based on extractor)
116     */
117    List<E> getSortedSliceList() {
118        return sliceQueue.stream()
119                .sorted(comparator)
120                .collect(Collectors.toList());
121    }
122
123    /**
124     * Get the total "slice size" of processed elements that are not saved in this accumulator.
125     *
126     * @return total slice size of other elements.
127     */
128    N getTotalOther() {
129        return otherTotal;
130    }
131
132    private N plus(N n1, N n2) {
133        return additionOperator.apply(n1, n2);
134    }
135}
